brief_justification: Correctly identifies C and states a valid core reason (trivial interconvertibility means no proof can privilege one constant); only surface-level analysis — does not explore what 'mathematical fundamentalness' means, why notational elegance fails as a criterion, or second-order implications; no reflection on reasoning method or potential biases at any point; no counterfactual checks, no boundary testing, no attempt to falsify the conclusion; acknowledges the aesthetic/pedagogical distinction but offers no structured uncertainty or confidence framing; never addresses why A, B, or D are incorrect, leaving all alternatives unexamined; states the conclusion with a one-sentence derivation but skips the intermediate step of explaining why interconvertibility entails a category boundary rather than mutual fundamentalness (option D).
